one_time_description: Gain on disposal of capital assets (machinery/equipment)
economic_notes: Band has investments in Lillooet Salish Enterprises Ltd. (50% ownership at cost), Bridge River Development Corporation (100% modified equity), and Bridge River Management LP (99.99% modified equity). BC Hydro revenue of $373,530 received. First Nation Gaming revenue of $372,758. Trust allocation of $372,282 from Ottawa Trust Fund.
notes: GBEs (Bridge River Development Corp and Bridge River Management LP) are accounted for using the modified equity method and are NOT fully consolidated — their assets/liabilities/revenues/expenses are excluded from consolidated totals. Restricted cash balance not separately stated on face of statements but referenced in notes (CMHC replacement reserve). Auditor firm name not explicitly named — office addresses listed in Nanaimo and White Rock BC. No schedule of remuneration included. total_government_transfers includes ISC $3,150,808 + CMHC $113,282 + Additional Government Funding $176,450 + DFO $43,034 + FNHA $810,543 = $4,293,117. OSR = $9,059,944 - $4,293,117 = $4,766,827. annual_debt_service reflects principal repayment on CMHC/ANTC loans per amortization schedule ($81,865 social housing amortization equals principal reduction per policy). CIBC builder's loan $321,796 proceeds received during year. Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
